locations.description: A 15-foot seasonal cascade at the base of the San Gabriel Mountains near Rancho Cucamonga, gateway to a system of beautiful canyons above Etiwanda.

locations.name: Sapphire Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall in the Big Bear area of the San Bernardino Mountains, set amid pine forest and granite boulders. Active primarily after winter snowmelt.
locations.location: Big Bear area, San Bernardino County

locations.name: Adobe Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall in Mission Trails Regional Park in San Diego, one of the few natural waterfalls within city limits. Active after winter rains and draining quickly by spring.
locations.location: Mission Trails Regional Park, San Diego County

locations.name: Cedar Creek Falls
locations.description: An 80-foot waterfall near Ramona in the San Diego backcountry, with a natural pool at the base popular for swimming. A permit is required; the 5.5-mile round-trip trail is gorgeous.
locations.location: Cleveland National Forest, Ramona, San Diego County

locations.name: Kitchen Creek Falls
locations.description: A 120-foot waterfall along the Pacific Crest Trail in Cleveland National Forest, one of the most accessible PCT waterfall sights in Southern California. The trailhead is off I-8 east of San Diego.
locations.location: Cleveland National Forest, San Diego County

locations.name: Three Sisters Falls
locations.description: A 160-foot three-tiered waterfall on Cedar Creek, the most dramatic waterfall in San Diego County. The 3-mile round trip involves significant elevation loss and gain with creek crossings.
locations.location: Cedar Creek, Cleveland National Forest, San Diego County

locations.name: Big Falls
locations.description: A 135-foot seasonal waterfall in the Santa Lucia Range of Los Padres National Forest. Active during the rainy season, the falls pour into a broad pool.
locations.location: Los Padres National Forest, San Luis Obispo County

locations.name: Nacimiento Falls
locations.description: A 20-foot cascade on the upper Nacimiento River in Los Padres National Forest, in a region known for its wild rivers and California condor habitat.
locations.location: Los Padres National Forest, San Luis Obispo County

locations.name: Mission Falls
locations.description: A seasonal cascade in Mission Canyon above Santa Barbara, fed by the drainage of the Santa Ynez Mountains. Part of a network of popular hiking trails above the city.

locations.name: Nojoqui Falls
locations.description: An 80-foot seasonal waterfall in Nojoqui Falls County Park near Solvang, tumbling over a moss-covered travertine cliff. A short half-mile trail leads to the base — one of the most easily accessible waterfalls on the Central Coast.
locations.location: Nojoqui Falls County Park, Santa Barbara County

locations.name: Rattlesnake Falls
locations.description: A seasonal cascade in Rattlesnake Canyon above Santa Barbara. Part of the popular Rattlesnake Canyon Trail, which winds through chaparral into the Santa Ynez Mountains.

locations.name: San Ysidro Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall above Montecito at the head of San Ysidro Canyon. The 8-mile round trip passes through one of the lushest canyons in the Santa Barbara area.
locations.location: San Ysidro Canyon, Montecito, Santa Barbara County

locations.name: Seven Falls
locations.description: A series of tiered cascades in Mission Canyon above Santa Barbara, popular for hiking and swimming. The boulder-scramble approach rewards visitors with a sequence of small falls and pools.

locations.name: Sisquoc Falls
locations.description: A 250-foot waterfall deep in the San Rafael Wilderness, one of the largest wilderness areas in California. A multi-day backpacking trip is required to reach this remote gem.
locations.location: San Rafael Wilderness, Los Padres National Forest, Santa Barbara County

locations.name: Tangerine Falls
locations.description: A 100-foot waterfall in Cold Springs Canyon above Santa Barbara, known for the orange lichen on its travertine walls — the source of its colorful name. The 5-mile round trip is a local favorite.
locations.location: Cold Springs Canyon, Santa Barbara County

locations.name: La Jolla Canyon Falls
locations.description: A 15-foot seasonal cascade in La Jolla Canyon at Point Mugu State Park, framed by dramatic volcanic rock formations. Part of a loop trail through the Mugu Peak area.
locations.location: Point Mugu State Park, Ventura County

locations.name: Matilija Falls
locations.description: A 40-foot cascade in Matilija Canyon above Ojai, a beloved spot for locals who brave the stream crossings to reach it. The canyon is home to the rare Matilija poppy, California's largest native wildflower.

locations.name: Paradise Falls
locations.description: A 70-foot year-round waterfall in Wildwood Regional Park in Thousand Oaks, one of the most accessible waterfalls in Ventura County. The 3-mile loop trail is popular with families.
locations.location: Wildwood Regional Park, Thousand Oaks, Ventura County

locations.name: Piru Creek Falls
locations.description: A 15-foot seasonal waterfall on Piru Creek in Los Padres National Forest. The creek runs through a beautiful canyon above Pyramid Lake.
locations.location: Piru Creek, Los Padres National Forest, Ventura County

locations.name: Rose Valley Falls
locations.description: A 300-foot tiered waterfall in Rose Valley in Los Padres National Forest above Ojai, one of the tallest waterfalls in the Transverse Ranges. A moderate 4-mile round trip leads to the base.
locations.location: Rose Valley, Los Padres National Forest, Ventura County

locations.name: Santa Paula Canyon Falls
locations.description: A 30-foot waterfall and swimming hole in Santa Paula Canyon, locally known as 'The Punch Bowls.' The trailhead at Thomas Aquinas College requires a weekday permit, but the canyon is one of Ventura County's finest.

locations.name: Santa Ynez Falls
locations.description: A seasonal cascade in Topanga Canyon, a lush riparian oasis in the Santa Monica Mountains. Active after winter rains and draining quickly in the dry season.

locations.name: Solstice Canyon Falls
locations.description: A 25-foot seasonal waterfall at the head of Solstice Canyon in the Santa Monica Mountains. The easy 2.5-mile round trip passes the ruins of a burned 1950s ranch house.
locations.location: Solstice Canyon, Santa Monica Mountains NRA, Ventura County

locations.name: Tar Creek Falls
locations.description: A 70-foot waterfall on Tar Creek in the Sespe Wilderness above Ojai. The remote canyon is part of the largest wilderness area in Los Padres National Forest — California condors soar overhead.
locations.location: Sespe Wilderness, Los Padres National Forest, Ventura County

locations.name: Chamberlain Creek Falls
locations.description: A 30-foot waterfall in Mendocino National Forest, accessible off Hwy 162. The falls drop into a shaded grotto — a hidden gem in the Coast Ranges.

locations.name: Russian Gulch Falls
locations.description: A 36-foot waterfall in Russian Gulch State Park near Mendocino, surrounded by a fern-draped second-growth redwood canyon. The 6-mile round trip is one of the Mendocino coast's finest hikes.
locations.location: Russian Gulch State Park, Mendocino County

locations.name: Zim Zim Falls
locations.description: A 105-foot waterfall in the remote Zim Zim Creek drainage of Napa County's Lake Berryessa backcountry. One of the larger waterfalls in the North Bay, requiring a rugged 9-mile round trip.

locations.name: Bowman Lake Falls
locations.description: An 80-foot waterfall near Bowman Lake in Tahoe National Forest, flowing from the high alpine terrain of the Grouse Ridge area. Reached via forest roads that may require high clearance.

locations.name: East Snow Mountain Falls
locations.description: A dramatic 2,200-foot cascade on East Snow Mountain in the Snow Mountain Wilderness, one of the tallest waterfalls in the Northern Coast Ranges. The remote wilderness peak is the highest point in Mendocino National Forest.
locations.location: Snow Mountain Wilderness, Mendocino National Forest, Lake County

locations.name: Grouse Falls
locations.description: A spectacular 504-foot free-falling waterfall in Tahoe National Forest near Foresthill, one of the tallest free-leaping falls in California. A short 1-mile trail leads to a viewpoint across the canyon.

locations.name: Hidden Falls
locations.description: A 30-foot waterfall in Hidden Falls Regional Park near Auburn, the centerpiece of a 30-mile trail network in the Sierra Nevada foothills. A popular year-round hiking destination for the Sacramento region.
locations.location: Hidden Falls Regional Park, Auburn, Placer County

locations.name: Frazier Falls
locations.description: A 178-foot waterfall near Gold Lake in Plumas National Forest, one of Northern California's most rewarding short hikes. A paved one-mile loop reaches a broad platform above this magnificent falls.
locations.location: Plumas National Forest near Gold Lake, Plumas County

locations.name: Indian Falls
locations.description: A 15-foot waterfall on the North Fork Feather River near Belden, visible from Hwy 89 in the Feather River Canyon. A scenic pullout makes this one of the most accessible falls in Plumas County.

locations.name: Burney Falls
locations.description: A magnificent 129-foot waterfall at McArthur-Burney Falls Memorial State Park, fed year-round by underground springs regardless of season. President Theodore Roosevelt reportedly called it the 'eighth wonder of the world.'
locations.location: McArthur-Burney Falls Memorial State Park, Shasta County

locations.name: Kings Creek Falls
locations.description: A 46-foot waterfall on Kings Creek in Lassen Volcanic National Park, reached via the 2.5-mile Kings Creek Falls Trail through meadows and forest below Lassen Peak.
locations.location: Lassen Volcanic National Park, Shasta County

locations.name: Pit River Falls
locations.description: A 50-foot cascade on the wild Pit River in Shasta County, a stretch prized by kayakers for its challenging whitewater. Remote and accessible via rough roads.

locations.name: Potem Falls
locations.description: A 70-foot waterfall on Squaw Creek near its confluence with the Sacramento River arm of Shasta Lake. Accessible via a short hike through chaparral.

locations.name: The Cascades
locations.description: A 198-foot multi-tiered cascade on Kings Creek in Lassen Volcanic National Park, just upstream of Kings Creek Falls. The entire Kings Creek corridor offers exceptional waterfall scenery in an active volcanic landscape.
locations.location: Lassen Volcanic National Park, Shasta County

locations.name: Whiskeytown Falls
locations.description: A 180-foot waterfall at Whiskeytown National Recreation Area near Redding, discovered to be one of California's tallest after GPS mapping in 2005. The 8-mile round-trip trail is surprisingly rugged for an NRA setting.
locations.location: Whiskeytown National Recreation Area, Shasta County

locations.name: Hedge Creek Falls
locations.description: A 30-foot waterfall in Dunsmuir that you can walk behind — a basalt cave behind the curtain of water is one of the most unique waterfall experiences in Northern California. Just a short half-mile trail from the city park.

locations.name: Ishi Pishi Falls
locations.description: A powerful seasonal cascade on the Klamath River, a sacred fishing site of the Karuk Tribe where they dip-net for salmon and steelhead. One of the most significant traditional fishing sites on the West Coast.

locations.name: McCloud River Falls
locations.description: The upper of three spectacular waterfalls on the McCloud River near McCloud, an 80-foot plunge into a turquoise pool. The 4-mile trail accesses Lower, Middle, and Upper McCloud Falls in sequence.

locations.name: Mossbrae Falls
locations.description: A curtain of spring water flowing from the hillside directly into the Sacramento River at Dunsmuir, creating an ethereal veil effect. Access requires a trail walk along active railroad tracks.

locations.name: Mud Creek Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall on the flanks of Mount Shasta, fed by snowmelt from the massive stratovolcano. Remote terrain requires significant hiking to approach.

locations.name: Whitney Falls
locations.description: A 200-foot waterfall on the north flank of Mount Shasta, flowing from the Whitney Glacier — one of the southernmost glaciers in the US. The 8-mile round trip also offers close-up views of volcanic fumaroles.
locations.location: North flank of Mount Shasta, Siskiyou County

locations.name: Angel Falls
locations.description: A 75-foot waterfall on Willow Creek in Sierra National Forest above Bass Lake, named for its ethereal appearance when backlit by afternoon sun.

locations.name: Chiquito Creek Falls
locations.description: A 70-foot cascade on Chiquito Creek in Sierra National Forest, in a remote stretch of the watershed between the main Sierra crest and the San Joaquin River drainage.

locations.name: Silver Pass Falls
locations.description: A 475-foot backcountry waterfall near Silver Pass on the John Muir Trail in the John Muir Wilderness. Encountered by JMT through-hikers during the high-country traverse between Mammoth and Kings Canyon.

locations.name: Silver Spray Falls
locations.description: A 200-foot waterfall in the Kings Canyon backcountry, its spray catching the light in the deep granite canyons of the southern Sierra Nevada.
locations.location: Kings Canyon National Park backcountry, Fresno County

locations.name: Stevenson Creek Falls
locations.description: A massive 1,200-foot waterfall on Stevenson Creek in Sierra National Forest — one of the tallest in the Sierra Nevada. The creek drains the high plateau above Shaver Lake before dropping dramatically toward the San Joaquin River.

locations.name: Chagoopa Falls
locations.description: A 1,200-foot waterfall on the remote Chagoopa Plateau in Sequoia National Park, one of the most spectacular and least-visited falls in the entire Sierra Nevada. A multi-day backpacking trip is required.
locations.location: Sequoia National Park backcountry, Tulare County

locations.name: Crystal Creek Falls
locations.description: A waterfall on Crystal Creek in Sequoia National Park, draining the high alpine terrain of the Great Western Divide. Located in a seldom-visited area of the park.

locations.name: Freeman Creek Falls
locations.description: A 150-foot waterfall on Freeman Creek in the Tule River watershed of Sequoia National Forest. The nearby Freeman Creek Grove contains some of the southernmost giant sequoias.

locations.name: Peppermint Creek Falls
locations.description: A 150-foot waterfall on Peppermint Creek in Sequoia National Forest, in a rugged canyon above the Kern River plateau. A wild and remote corner of the southern Sierra.

locations.name: Salmon Creek Falls
locations.description: A massive 450-foot waterfall on Salmon Creek in the remote southern Sierra, not to be confused with the Monterey County namesake. One of the tallest falls in Sequoia National Forest, requiring a substantial backcountry approach.

locations.name: Three Falls Below The Gate
locations.description: A series of 200-foot cascades on the upper Kaweah River drainage in Sequoia National Park, accessible to those venturing beyond the standard backcountry routes.

locations.name: Volcano Falls
locations.description: A waterfall in the volcanic geology of the southern Sierra Nevada foothills in Sequoia National Forest, near the transition zone between the granite Sierra and Kern River country.

locations.name: Niagara Creek Falls
locations.description: A 730-foot waterfall on Niagara Creek in the Grand Canyon of the Tuolumne watershed, a remote and seldom-visited cascade in the high Yosemite backcountry.
locations.location: Yosemite National Park backcountry, Tuolumne County

locations.name: Aspen Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall near aspen groves in Mono County's high Sierra terrain, active during snowmelt and early summer.

locations.name: Ellery Lake Falls
locations.description: A seasonal cascade near Ellery Lake on the eastern approach to Tioga Pass. One of several waterfalls active during summer snowmelt along Hwy 120's eastern corridor.

locations.name: Gem Lake Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall feeding Gem Lake in the Rush Creek drainage of the Ansel Adams Wilderness. Part of the spectacular high-country trail system above June Lake.

locations.name: Rush Creek Falls
locations.description: A 200-foot cascade on Rush Creek above June Lake, flowing from the high Sierra toward Mono Lake. The Rush Creek drainage is one of the most spectacular in the Eastern Sierra.

locations.name: Fox Creek Falls
locations.description: A 60-foot seasonal waterfall on Fox Creek in the San Gabriel Mountains near Sierra Madre. Tucked in a wooded canyon accessible from the Big Santa Anita Canyon trail system.
locations.location: Angeles National Forest, Los Angeles County

locations.name: Grand Chasm Falls
locations.description: A seasonal waterfall in the Grand Chasm of the San Gabriel Mountains, reached via a demanding off-trail route. The dramatic gorge makes the approach as memorable as the falls themselves.
locations.location: San Gabriel Mountains, Angeles National Forest, Los Angeles County

locations.name: Thalehaha Falls
locations.description: An 80-foot waterfall in the San Gabriel Mountains, whose name is said to derive from a Tongva word. Located in the network of canyons above Altadena.
locations.location: San Gabriel Mountains, Los Angeles County

locations.name: Wolfskill Falls
locations.description: A 100-foot seasonal waterfall in the San Gabriel foothills near Glendora, named for early California settler William Wolfskill, who planted the region's first orange grove in the 1840s.
locations.location: San Gabriel Mountains foothills, Los Angeles County

locations.name: Falls Canyon Falls
locations.description: A 30-foot seasonal waterfall in Falls Canyon in the Santa Ana Mountains of Orange County, flowing after winter rains into a small pool.

locations.name: Laurel Canyon Falls
locations.description: A 100-foot seasonal waterfall in Laurel Canyon in the Santa Ana Mountains, one of the taller falls in Orange County. The narrow canyon stays shaded and cool long after surrounding hills dry out.

locations.name: Frustration Creek Falls
locations.description: A 60-foot waterfall on Frustration Creek in the San Bernardino Mountains — the name reflects the challenging off-trail approach through brush-choked terrain.
locations.location: San Bernardino Mountains, San Bernardino County

locations.name: Monkeyface Falls
locations.description: A 100-foot seasonal waterfall in the San Bernardino Mountains, named for a distinctive rock formation near the falls that resembles a primate's face.
locations.location: San Bernardino Mountains, San Bernardino County

locations.name: Ash Creek Falls
locations.description: A 290-foot waterfall on Ash Creek in the volcanic terrain near Lassen Volcanic National Park. The volcanic geology of the Cascades gives this falls a dramatic setting of dark lava flows and ash deposits.
